title: toona sinensis var. grandifolia
intro: Toona sinensis var. grandifolia, also known as the Chinese toona or Chinese mahogany, is a variety of the Toona sinensis plant, a deciduous tree native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Toona sinensis var. grandifolia include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and sore throats, as well as its potential in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
